extra.reason: Delivered by initial scaffold: cargo workspace (core+cli), CircuitSource + Stage traits with tests, lob CLI skeleton, Rust .gitignore, rustfmt.toml, documented dev loop. All gates green (check/test/build/fmt/clippy).

Delivered by initial scaffold: cargo workspace (core+cli), CircuitSource + Stage traits with tests, lob CLI skeleton, Rust .gitignore, rustfmt.toml, documented dev loop. All gates green (check/test/build/fmt/clippy).
All foundation tasks complete; scaffold builds and the trait boundaries are in place.

Toolchain pinned + documented (docs/TOOLING.md, requirements.txt), tool discovery in core::tools, 'lob doctor' preflight implemented and verified; RC low-pass SKiDL circuit emits a clean netlist end-to-end.

Toolchain pinned + documented (docs/TOOLING.md, requirements.txt), tool discovery in core::tools, 'lob doctor' preflight implemented and verified; RC low-pass SKiDL circuit emits a clean netlist end-to-end.

SKiDL runner (core::skidl::SkidlRunner) shells out via the venv python, runs in a scratch work_dir so artifacts stay out of the repo, captures netlist + ERC report, surfaces missing-interpreter/non-zero-exit as StageError (not panic). Wired as stage 1 of 'lob run'; verified end-to-end on examples/rc_lowpass.py. 10 unit tests, fmt+clippy clean.

core::netlist parses the KiCad S-expr netlist into the Circuit model (first CircuitSource producer): parts (refdes/value/footprint/libsource) + nets (name + pins), deterministically sorted. Self-contained S-expr reader, no new deps. Wired as stage 2 of 'lob run' (parses the real generated netlist: 2 parts, 3 nets). 14 unit tests incl. round-trip on an RC netlist fixture; fmt+clippy clean.

core::spice generates a SPICE deck from the Circuit (pin→net inversion, GND→0, R/C/L support, IN/OUT/GND harness), runs 'ngspice -b', and parses the AC response (freq, vdb). AcResult exposes passband gain + interpolated -3dB cutoff. Verified: 601-point sweep on the RC filter. 9 spice/units tests.

core::verify::check_rc_cutoff computes analytic 1/(2piRC) from parsed R/C values and asserts the simulated -3dB cutoff within a configurable tolerance. End-to-end: expected 1000.97 Hz vs simulated 1000.96 Hz (0.001% err). Failure path unit-tested (wrong cutoff → failed outcome w/ diff). Non-single-RC → skipped.

Completed the 6-stage Phase 0 loop. validate (core::validate): surfaces ERC WARNING/ERROR lines as structured findings, fails only on ERC errors. bom (core::bom): groups parts by value+footprint, qty+refdes, CSV + aligned table, flags missing footprints. Wired into 'lob run' in DESIGN order skidl→parse→validate→simulate→verify→bom with per-stage report + non-zero exit. Verified end-to-end on RC filter: all 6 stages pass, exit 0, BOM CSV written. 30 core tests, fmt+clippy clean.

Wired into 'lob run' in DESIGN order skidl→parse→validate→simulate→verify→bom with per-stage report + non-zero exit. Verified end-to-end on RC filter: all 6 stages pass, exit 0, BOM CSV written. 30 core tests, fmt+clippy clean.

Completed the 6-stage Phase 0 loop. validate (core::validate): surfaces ERC WARNING/ERROR lines as structured findings, fails only on ERC errors. bom (core::bom): groups parts by value+footprint, qty+refdes, CSV + aligned table, flags missing footprints. Wired into 'lob run' in DESIGN order skidl→parse→validate→simulate→verify→bom with per-stage report + non-zero exit. Verified end-to-end on RC filter: all 6 stages pass, exit 0, BOM CSV written. 30 core tests, fmt+clippy clean.

Implemented: components carry their SPICE model, resolved FROM the component (core::symbols reads the KiCad symbol's Sim.Device/Name/Library/Pins — which SKiDL's netlist drops — and the .sp subckt terminal order). core::spice::SpiceModel + generic generate_ac_deck instantiates subckt (X-line + .include) or primitive (R/C/L) with supply rails, ZERO per-device conditionals. Verified: RC via primitive path, op-amp via the real kicad_builtin_opamp subckt, both through the same generic path. S-expr reader promoted to shared core::sexpr.

Non-inverting op-amp gain stage (examples/opamp_noninv.py, Rf=9k/Rg=1k -> gain 10) passes the full 6-stage loop: sim uses the component's real op-amp subckt (resolved from the symbol, not hardcoded), verify checks 1+Rf/Rg topologically (expected 10.000x / 20dB, simulated 9.995x -> 0.05% err, the real op-amp's finite-gain deviation). Second demo circuit exercises the loop on a different topology + active device. Built on okm.8.

Phase 0 complete: the full 6-stage loop (SKiDL -> parse -> validate -> simulate -> verify -> BOM) is proven end-to-end on two textbook circuits with known-good answers — RC low-pass (cutoff vs 1/2piRC, 0.001%) and non-inverting op-amp (gain vs 1+Rf/Rg, 0.05% via the component's real subckt model). Local-only, self-verifying, non-zero exit on failure. Stretch task P0.11 (layout scaffolding) superseded by the Layout epic's kicad-ipc-rs task (j54.5).

Dolt-backed parts library schema + read/write interface shipped. core::parts: parts/part_pins/part_ratings (mpn as natural key, citations via cited_page); PartsLibrary shells out to the dolt CLI (init + schema + transactional upsert + JSON reads + versioning commit), matching the project's shell-out pattern. PartRecord/PinRecord/RatingRecord, verified_by_human gate field, default cross-project dir (LOB_PARTS_DIR override). 'lob parts list/show/add/verify' CLI. Verified end-to-end (LM13700) + unit + real-dolt round-trip test. Unblocks okm.3 (fetch_datasheet), okm.7 (MPN resolution), okm.9 (SPICE models).

MPN resolution shipped. model::Part carries mpn (extracted from the netlist's MPN field); PartsLibrary::resolve_circuit returns per-part ResolutionStatus (NoMpn/Unknown/Unverified/Verified) against the library; 'lob parts resolve <circuit>'. Verified end-to-end: a SKiDL circuit with MPN=LM13700 resolves ⚠ unverified → ✓ verified. Unblocks zya.2 (Mouser pricing by resolved MPN) and okm.4 (verification gate). 41 core tests.

Mouser-priced BOM delivered end-to-end. zya.1: BomLine carries mpn+unit_price+ext_price, grouped by (mpn,value,footprint), Bom::total(), priced CSV+table. zya.2: core::mouser thin ureq client — search_mpn->PartPrice (matched MPN, stock, qty price breaks), unit_price_at, MOUSER_API_KEY from .env, response parsing unit-tested. zya.3: 'lob bom <circuit> --price --out <csv>'. Verified against LIVE Mouser API: LM13700M/NOPB priced .56 (1316 in stock) -> ext+total+CSV. 45 core tests.

Mouser-priced BOM delivered end-to-end. zya.1: BomLine carries mpn+unit_price+ext_price, grouped by (mpn,value,footprint), Bom::total(), priced CSV+table. zya.2: core::mouser thin ureq client — search_mpn->PartPrice (matched MPN, stock, qty price breaks), unit_price_at, MOUSER_API_KEY from .env, response parsing unit-tested. zya.3: 'lob bom <circuit> --price --out <csv>'. Verified against LIVE Mouser API: LM13700M/NOPB priced .56 (1316 in stock) -> ext+total+CSV. 45 core tests.

Mouser-priced BOM delivered end-to-end. zya.1: BomLine carries mpn+unit_price+ext_price, grouped by (mpn,value,footprint), Bom::total(), priced CSV+table. zya.2: core::mouser thin ureq client — search_mpn->PartPrice (matched MPN, stock, qty price breaks), unit_price_at, MOUSER_API_KEY from .env, response parsing unit-tested. zya.3: 'lob bom <circuit> --price --out <csv>'. Verified against LIVE Mouser API: LM13700M/NOPB priced .56 (1316 in stock) -> ext+total+CSV. 45 core tests.

fetch_datasheet delivered across two structured sources: KiCad official library (core::fetch::fetch_from_kicad — pins + datasheet URL) + JLCPCB distributor API (core::jlcpcb HMAC-signed, verified live — datasheet + structured ratings + MPN by LCSC code). Records keyed by MPN, unverified until confirmed; fetches MERGE across sources (CLI merge_fetched) so pins+ratings compose. PDF-page-citation extraction tier tracked as legion-of-bom-okm.11.

Distributor/CAD sourcing + trust order implemented: KiCad official CAD library + JLCPCB distributor API (per the DESIGN 3.5 trust order). Import-tooling directive (drive KiCad PCM / JLC2KICAD_lib per LIBRARIES.md) documented in notes. PDF fallback split to legion-of-bom-okm.11.

Verification gate shipped: PartResolution::blocks_verified_use + 'lob parts gate <circuit>' exits non-zero if any MPN-bearing part isn't human-verified — the enforceable structural block that layout/real-BOM-ordering call. Verified fail(exit1)->pass.

Human verification workflow: 'lob parts show <mpn>' presents extracted pins/ratings/datasheet + status; 'lob parts verify <mpn> --by' confirms ONCE per part (global, reused across projects). Tool-enforced version of the slew-limiter open-questions checklist.

Policy decided (LIBRARIES.md): a library's license is informational metadata surfaced to the user, NOT a pipeline gate — legion-of-bom doesn't police end-user usage. Only exposure is redistribution (CC-BY-SA 'collection' clause if we bundle library files). Verify a source before vouching (KiCad official confirmed CC-BY-SA 4.0). No license enforcement in the pipeline.

Board generation shipped: core::board::generate_board (netlist → valid .kicad_pcb, headless, direct S-expr). Loads .kicad_mod footprints, places (Placer trait — GridPlacer default, loop/PanelSpec placers plug in later) + net-wires them, 2-layer stack + net table. Deterministic UUIDs (sha2) → clean git diffs. core::sexpr rewritten as reader+writer (Sym/Str). 'lob board <circuit>'. Verified: kicad-cli reads it (DRC runs, nets wired) + exports full gerber set. Remaining board completeness is OTHER tasks: routing (j54.6 loop), ground pour (j54.2), board outline (PanelSpec j54.10). 54 core tests.

Board outline (Edge.Cuts rect from parts bbox + margin) + bottom-layer ground pour (B.Cu zone flooded to GND, DESIGN 6.2 default). Emitted via the generate_board seam; kicad-cli fills the zone on 'export gerbers --check-zones' (B.Cu gerber 459->965 bytes). Outline took DRC 1->0 violations. BoardOptions.ground_net/outline_margin_mm. Real panel-driven outline comes with PanelSpec (j54.10).
Two-layer obstacle-aware maze router (GridRouter) + Router seam; routes both demo circuits DRC-clean (0 violations, 0 unconnected), incl. vias on the RC ladder

Real TL072 flows through the whole pipeline (sim 20dB, verify gain 9.995x, BOM MPN, board routes DRC 0/0). Model travels with the part (Sim.* fields -> netlist -> resolver). Removed the is_opamp/is_ideal_opamp device-sniffing anti-pattern: analytic_check is now a registry of self-selecting checks. Added size-aware placement so real ICs don't collide.

Double-sided via explicit per-part Side declaration (default front). Back parts flip correctly (layers/coords/text), validated by render + DRC 0 errors. Mutable single-sided and Super Synthesis double-sided both handled.

Router now treats through-hole pads as free layer transitions (PadLayer::layers): a pad is a source/target on every layer it touches, so the router meets a through-hole pad on whichever side the net is already on instead of vias-ing onto its hole. double_sided.py hole_to_hole warnings 4->0; all boards 0 errors.

DRC readback via headless kicad-cli (not IPC — same de-risk as direct-gen). core::drc: run_drc shells kicad-cli pcb drc --format json --refill-zones, parses into DrcReport {violations/unconnected/parity} with severity helpers (errors/warnings/is_clean). CLI: lob drc <board> reports + exits non-zero on errors. This is the layout loop's check step (6.5 step 3). Surfaced a real latent issue (isolated GND pour -> j54.17 stitching).

lob fab: DRC-gated manufacturing package. core::fab exports Gerbers+drill (kicad-cli), auto-zips them for JLCPCB PCB upload, writes JLC-format CPL (Designator,Mid X,Mid Y,Layer,Rotation) reformatted from kicad pos, and JLC-format BOM (Comment,Designator,Footprint,LCSC) from generate_bom. Gated on run_drc (j54.11) — refuses to package a board with errors. Validated on opamp + double_sided: full gerber zip + CPL + BOM.
Done: aux_axis_origin at outline bottom-left; --use-drill-file-origin on pos+gerbers, --drill-origin plot on drill. Verified: opamp CPL R1 at (6.425, 7.205) instead of (100, -100).
